# GasMath

> GasMath finds the single most cost-effective gas station for you right now — factoring live station prices, real driving distance, your vehicle's fuel economy, and how much gas you actually need. It returns one decision, not a directory.

GasMath is a privacy-first progressive web app (PWA). There are no accounts and no sign-in, and no user data is stored on its servers — all personalization (vehicle, warehouse-club membership, Top Tier preference, fuel grade) lives only in the browser's local storage. Your location is used transiently to find nearby stations and is never stored.

The verdict screen compares two stations so the tradeoff is explicit: your **closest / most efficient** station (the one that burns the least fuel to reach) and your **cheapest** station (lowest true total cost), with the dollar savings of driving to the cheaper one.

## How the decision works
- Effective cost = (gallons needed + fuel burned driving there) × station price
- Gallons needed comes from a fuel-gauge slider × the vehicle's tank capacity
- The winner is the lowest effective cost; ties within $0.05 go to the nearer station
- Warehouse-club stations (Costco, BJ's, Sam's Club) appear only for members
- Optional Top Tier–only filter; prices older than 12 hours are excluded

## Data sources
- Google Places API (New) — station locations and live fuel prices
- OpenRouteService — real driving distances
- EPA fuel-economy data — vehicle combined MPG (merged with a curated tank-capacity table)

## Pages
- [GasMath app](https://gasmath.app/): the tool itself
- [Privacy Policy](https://gasmath.app/privacy.html): local-only storage, transient geolocation, third-party services
- [Terms of Use](https://gasmath.app/terms.html): terms and accuracy disclaimers

## Notes
- No advertising and no selling of data.
- Estimates can be inaccurate, especially for hybrid and plug-in hybrid vehicles; always verify the posted price at the pump.
